title: BASIC mode — compare
description: Same deal as 'L2 mode — full audit' (qty=50000, budget=₹420), but declares BASIC_SALES_QUOTING_1 as the expected seller-response-mode. To actually run in BASIC mode you must set SELLER_RESPONSE_MODE=BASIC_SALES_QUOTING_1 in A2A/js/src/agents/seller-agent/.env and restart the seller (no hot reload — by design, so audit can't have ambiguous mode). In BASIC mode the seller's path is: LLM proposal → applySellerConstraints (hard floor at ₹350+5) → applyTreasuryConstraint. NO multi-advisor consultation, NO L2 executive synthesis. Audit will have an EMPTY 'l2' extras block. Use this scenario together with 'L2 mode — full audit' to see the audit-side difference between the two modes for an identical deal.

expectedOutcome.likely: 3-round close at ~₹375–₹395/unit (same closing band as L2 — the price outcome is similar but the AUDIT differs). Audit JSON: no consultations[], no tacticsTrace[], no mathOverrides[], no defensiveActions[] under extras.l2 (empty {}).
expectedOutcome.possible: If SELLER_RESPONSE_MODE in .env is NOT set to BASIC_SALES_QUOTING_1, the scenario will still run, but the seller will execute whatever mode IS configured — likely L2 — and the audit will NOT match this card's expectation. Check the UI Settings card's 'Active' mode before running.
expectedOutcome.failureMode: There is no behavioral wire today from scenario.sellerIntent.hardConstraints.sellerResponseMode to the seller agent. The scenario card DECLARES the expected mode; the .env CONFIGURES the actual mode. Aligning them is the operator's job until the seller-intent envelope wire lands (FRAMEWORK-V2 §12 D7 deferred work).

declaredButDeferred: sellerIntent (other fields — seller acts on its own .env config)
